authoritative_sources.title: Interoperability and Prior Authorization
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS is the federal regulator for covered Medicare Advantage, Medicaid, CHIP, and QHP issuer prior authorization interoperability requirements. The source directly governs payer authorization status exchange, payer-provider operational workflow expectations, and prior authorization evidence dependencies.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Authorization intake, prerequisite validation, provider response tracking, and authorization status routing
used_for: authorization prerequisite checks
payer-provider authorization exchange terminology
refusal when authorization approval or medical necessity decision authority is requested

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Claims Processing Manual
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS publishes the official Medicare claims processing manual. It is authoritative for claim processing, adjustment, correction, remittance, and source-evidence concepts that constrain payer-side claims inventory and provider inquiry dispositions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Claims inventory, pend handling, claim status, correction, remittance, and closeout
used_for: claim state and evidence vocabulary
claim pend, correction, adjustment, and remittance dependency handling
refusal to force adjudication or invent claim outcome

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Managed Care Manual, Chapter 13 - Medicare Managed Care Beneficiary Grievances, Organization Determinations, and Appeals
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS managed-care appeal guidance defines organization determinations, grievances, appeals, timeframes, notices, and formal decision paths. It is authoritative for the handoff boundary between payer operations support and formal appeal or determination authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Member-impacting escalation, adverse determination routing, grievance or appeal intake support
used_for: appeal and grievance boundary rules
member-rights sensitive routing
refusal to decide final appeal, grievance, organization determination, or benefit interpretation

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR Part 422 Subpart M - Grievances, Organization Determinations and Appeals
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR is the continuously updated public codification of federal regulations. Subpart M is the federal regulatory baseline for Medicare Advantage grievance, organization-determination, and appeal processes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Regulated member-rights escalation and formal determination boundary
used_for: regulated appeal and grievance escalation triggers
notice-sensitive member impact handling
conflict escalation when product or tenant policy differs from federal baseline

authoritative_sources.title: Administrative Simplification: Transactions
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS administers HIPAA administrative simplification transaction standards. This source is authoritative for eligibility, claim status, claim or encounter, payment, and remittance transaction expectations used by payer operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Eligibility, claim status, claim encounter, payment, and remittance transaction evidence handling
used_for: eligibility and claim status prerequisite checks
transaction terminology and source evidence expectations
refusal when transaction evidence and system records conflict

authoritative_sources.title: Health Care Payment and Remittance Advice and Electronic Funds Transfer
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS administrative simplification guidance is authoritative for ERA, EFT, payment, remittance, CARC, RARC, and reassociation concepts used when payer operations responds to provider inquiries and member-impacting claim questions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Remittance, payment, denial reason, and provider inquiry reconciliation
used_for: EOB, EOP, ERA, EFT, CARC, and RARC vocabulary
provider inquiry evidence handling
refusal to resolve payment conflicts without claims and remittance evidence

authoritative_sources.title: Uses and Disclosures for Treatment, Payment, and Health Care Operations
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR enforces HIPAA privacy rules. This guidance is authoritative for when payer operations may use or disclose PHI for payment and healthcare operations and when privacy review is required.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PHI use, disclosure, correspondence, and escalation handling
used_for: payment and healthcare operations disclosure checks
PHI-sensitive provider and member correspondence limits
privacy escalation when disclosure purpose or authority is unclear

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Necessary Requirement
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR minimum-necessary guidance constrains payer operations evidence packets, provider or member correspondence, and cross-queue escalation involving PHI.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Minimum-necessary evidence handling and audit-ready disclosure posture
used_for: PHI minimization in provider or member escalation summaries
approved channel requirements
refusal of uncontrolled disclosure requests

authoritative_sources.title: Operating Rules
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CAQH CORE operating rules are recognized national healthcare administrative operating rules for eligibility, claim status, prior authorization, payment, and remittance transactions used between providers and health plans.
used_for: eligibility, claim status, prior authorization, and remittance workflow vocabulary
provider inquiry and transaction-evidence expectations
source conflict handling when payer portal and transaction records disagree

authoritative_sources.title: Da Vinci Prior Authorization Support Implementation Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HL7 is a recognized healthcare interoperability standards organization. The Da Vinci PAS implementation guide directly models payer-provider prior authorization request and response workflows and evidence exchange terms.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Prior authorization request, response, attachment, and status workflow
used_for: authorization artifact and status terms
provider evidence exchange expectations
pend and missing-information scenario design

authoritative_sources.title: Utilization Management Accreditation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NCQA is a recognized health plan accreditation body. Its utilization-management materials support the boundary that UM decision-making and clinical review require defined clinical review authority, separate from payer operations queue handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Clinical review and utilization management boundary handling
used_for: medical necessity and UM decision boundary
clinical-review escalation scenarios
refusal to approve or deny authorization from operations authority

workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
workflow_questions.answer: Payer operations is gated by member identity, eligibility, active coverage span, plan and product, jurisdiction, service date, provider and facility facts, referral dependency, authorization status, clinical document inventory, provider response status, claim control number, claim state, policy source, queue SLA, PHI channel, delegated entity arrangement, and tenant source-of-truth hierarchy.

workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
workflow_questions.answer: The truth set is established by payer core administration, eligibility and benefit systems, provider directory, authorization management, utilization management system status, claims processing system, claim status transaction records, provider portal, CRM or call system, case management, EOB/EOP and remittance records, correspondence repository, policy repository, grievance and appeal system, exception log, and audit system.

workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops at administrative evidence sufficiency, lifecycle state classification, blocker identification, next-owner routing, and escalation packet preparation. Licensed clinical or UM owners decide medical necessity, level of care, diagnosis support, and authorization approval or denial. Privacy or compliance owners decide non-routine PHI disclosure and disputed consent questions. Claims adjudication, benefits, appeals, grievance, legal, finance, and compliance owners decide final claim outcomes, benefit interpretation, formal determinations, legal positions, and financial approvals.

workflow_stages.name: Payer work intake and identity validation
workflow_stages.description: Identify member, provider, plan, product, jurisdiction, service dates, requested action, source systems, case ID, claim or authorization ID, correspondence channel, urgency, and member-impact posture before work begins.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and authorization gate check
workflow_stages.description: Validate eligibility, benefit context, referral status, authorization requirements, provider response, clinical document inventory, policy source, and authorization management record before progression.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Claims inventory and provider inquiry processing
workflow_stages.description: Review claim control number, claim line state, pend reason, edits, claim status transactions, remittance, EOB or EOP, provider portal case, and correspondence to classify normal processing, pend, exception, rework, route, or completion.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Member-impacting queue and formal-rights screening
workflow_stages.description: Screen whether provider complaint, member correspondence, denied service, notice timing, adverse determination, appeal, or grievance indicators require handoff to formal appeals, compliance, member service, or legal owners.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception, conflict, and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when systems disagree, clinical or UM judgment is required, policy interpretation changes the outcome, PHI handling is disputed, or a requested action would force adjudication, benefit interpretation, appeal result, or closure without evidence.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Disposition, completion, and audit evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Close or route only when source evidence, queue state, correspondence, owner handoff, system update, PHI handling posture, and audit trail support the lifecycle state and completion claim.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

artifacts: authorization requests, service authorization numbers, referral records, utilization management status, clinical document inventory, and provider-submitted evidence
claim inventory, claim control numbers, claim line states, pend reasons, claim edits, EOBs, EOPs, ERA, EFT, CARC, RARC, denial or payment codes, and provider disputes
member eligibility, benefit records, accumulator context, plan and product facts, coverage spans, service dates, provider and facility facts, and jurisdiction context
provider inquiries, provider portal messages, member correspondence, policy notices, call or CRM notes, grievance or appeal intake artifacts, and case-management tasks
policy repository extracts, delegated entity policies, tenant SOPs, queue SLAs, routing matrix, exception logs, owner handoffs, correspondence proofs, and audit logs
systems_of_record: utilization management system for review status only
EOB, EOP, remittance, and payment systems
CRM, contact center, and case management systems
policy, product, contract, and delegated entity repositories
decision_boundaries: May classify payer work into accepted, blocked, pended, exceptioned, rework, routed, escalated, completed, or orchestrator-return states based on source evidence.
May determine which missing operational evidence, provider response, referral, authorization, eligibility, claim, correspondence, or policy dependency prevents completion.
May package escalation summaries for provider, member, authorization, claim, privacy, appeal, compliance, or clinical owners.
Must not approve or deny medical necessity, level of care, diagnosis support, clinical urgency, or authorization outcome.
Must not force claim adjudication, reverse denial, approve payment, determine benefit interpretation, finalize appeal or grievance, or override payer policy.
Must not disclose PHI outside minimum-necessary, role-based, tenant-approved payment or operations channels.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Missing provider or clinical evidence treated as sufficient
domain_failure_modes.risk: Incorrect authorization, pended claim release, denial, provider dispute, or member access delay.
domain_failure_modes.required_handling: Pend or exception the work, request missing evidence, and route clinical review or provider response ownership.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: PHI exposed in provider or member escalation
domain_failure_modes.risk: Privacy violation, unauthorized disclosure, incomplete audit trail, and compliance exposure.
domain_failure_modes.required_handling: Apply minimum necessary, use approved channels, and escalate disputed disclosure authority to privacy or compliance.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Operations lane forces claim adjudication or appeal outcome
domain_failure_modes.risk: Payment error, unsupported denial reversal, adverse member impact, regulatory exposure, and audit failure.
domain_failure_modes.required_handling: Refuse forced outcome and route to claims adjudication, appeals, UM, benefit policy, compliance, or legal owner.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Provider complaint, authorization, and claim queues are flattened into one owner
domain_failure_modes.risk: Wrong routing, duplicate work, missed timeframes, member harm, provider dissatisfaction, and source-record conflict.
domain_failure_modes.required_handling: Separate owner responsibilities, preserve a unified issue summary, and track each queue state and dependency.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Conflicting public, tenant, product, or delegated entity policy is resolved by assumption
domain_failure_modes.risk: Incorrect regulatory posture, inconsistent member rights handling, or product-specific error.
domain_failure_modes.required_handling: Record conflict and escalate for authorized interpretation while preserving source hierarchy.

required_domain_scenario_family_map.family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
A payer work item is missing required clinical or provider-side evidence
Provider complaint, authorization, and claims handling overlap and need correct routing
A user asks the lane to force an adjudication outcome without authority
Normal in-scope execution from accepted work to completion
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Tenant-specific adaptation that preserves public baseline

unresolved_ambiguity.description: Medicare Advantage, Medicaid managed care, commercial, exchange, ASO, delegated-risk, workers compensation, dental, vision, and pharmacy products can impose different timeframes, notices, benefit rules, and delegated owner paths. The pack records this as a mandatory tenant retrieval dependency rather than guessing.

Public sources cannot determine which tenant system controls when core administration, authorization, claims, provider portal, CRM, case management, and audit systems disagree. Runtime must retrieve source hierarchy before disposition.

research_to_spec_constraints: The spec requires explicit member, provider, product, jurisdiction, authorization, referral, claim, and source-system facts before action.
The spec treats medical necessity, authorization approval, appeal disposition, benefit interpretation, privacy approval, legal advice, and finance signoff as refusal or handoff boundaries.
The scenario suite includes missing prerequisite, PHI, clinical boundary, forced adjudication, provider complaint overlap, low-confidence, conflict, tenant adaptation, and completion evidence cases.
The final pack remains specification-based and requires tenant retrieval before runtime materialization.
